description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a wide-body fuselage and a distinctive hump on the upper deck, characteristic of the Boeing 747 series. The aircraft features four underwing turbofan engines, a swept low-wing configuration, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer. The livery is predominantly white with blue and gray stripes, and the tail displays a blue globe logo with an American flag. The landing gear is extended, showing a tricycle configuration with multiple wheels on each main gear. The cockpit is located at the forward section of the fuselage, above the nose, with multiple windows visible.
description: A large, white and blue wide-body commercial airplane with four underwing engines, a distinctive upper deck hump, and a conventional tail. The aircraft displays a blue globe logo and an American flag on the tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide fuselage, four underwing engines, and the swept wings extending outward.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the upper deck hump, cockpit windows, and detailed livery with the blue globe logo and American flag on the tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full wingspan, four engines, and the single vertical stabilizer of the tail.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the upper deck, engine placement, and tail markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept configuration with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows above the nose.
